message:
perf(http): optimize HTTP/1.1 codec with byte-level CRLF scanning and combined header validation

Replace the windows(4)-based header-end detection and windows(2)-based
CRLF scanning with dedicated byte-level search functions (find_headers_end,
find_crlf) that avoid iterator/slice overhead on the hot path. The previous
commit baf801f reverted to windows()-based scanning; this re-introduces
the optimization with a cleaner implementation.

Key changes:

- find_headers_end(): replaces buf.windows(4).position() with a direct
  byte-comparison loop scanning for \r\n\r\n, eliminating per-position
  slice allocation overhead
- find_crlf(): new helper replacing buf.windows(2).position() for \r\n
  scanning, used in split_line_crlf, decode_head, and chunk parsing
- parse_request_line_bytes(): new byte-level request line parser that
  avoids the intermediate UTF-8 string conversion for the method and
  version tokens, only converting the URI portion to str. Uses a manual
  cursor-based tokenizer (next_token_bounds) instead of split_ascii_whitespace
- transfer_and_content_length(): combined single-pass extraction of
  Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length headers with duplicate detection,
  replacing two separate unique_header_value() calls in both request
  decode and response encode paths
- decode_head(): reuse the request_line_end position computed during the
  length-limit check to avoid redundant CRLF scanning; work directly
  with byte slices instead of converting the entire header block to &str
- parse_header_line_bytes(): called with byte slices instead of &str

These changes target the HTTP/1.1 request parsing hot path where every
microsecond matters under high connection concurrency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
